description_long: This is a double-ended open wrench made of metal, likely steel, with a slightly weathered, darkened finish. Each end features a U-shaped open jaw, sized differently to accommodate various bolt or nut sizes. The shaft is straight and flat, with a subtle tapering toward the center, providing a comfortable grip for hand use. The surface shows some discoloration and minor wear, suggesting regular use in mechanical or construction settings. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, and the handle lacks any specialized grip pattern. The overall appearance is utilitarian, designed for durability and function in urban environments such as garages or workshops.
description: A double-ended open metal wrench with U-shaped jaws and a slightly worn, dark finish. The shaft is straight and flat, showing signs of regular use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wrench, both open jaws clearly visible at each end.
description_view_1: End-on view focusing on one open jaw, with the shaft appearing narrow and the jaw's U-shape prominent.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the full length and both jaws, with minor surface wear visible.
description_view_3: End-on view of the other jaw, showing the U-shape and the flatness of the shaft.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Minor discoloration and wear marks are visible along the shaft and near the jaws, indicating moderate use.
